2604.25376.title: CoRE: Concept-Reasoning Expansion for Continual Brain Lesion Segmentation
2604.25376.paper_summary: Accurate brain lesion segmentation in MRI is vital for effective clinical diagnosis and treatment planning. Due to high annotation costs and strict data privacy regulations, universal models require employing Continual Learning (CL) to adapt to evolving clinical tasks without losing previously acquired knowledge. However, existing CL paradigms often suffer from capacity limits or redundant parameter growth, and even advanced dynamic methods rely mostly on image-perception strategies that struggle to handle the substantial pathological and multimodal heterogeneity inherent in brain imaging. To address this issue, we propose Concept-Reasoning Expansion (CoRE) framework, which establishes a joint decision-making mechanism by integrating visual features with structured concepts. Through the alignment of image tokens with a hierarchical concept library, CoRE simulates clinical reasoning to guide both interpretable expert routing and demand-based model growth. This collaborative process ensures model evolution is grounded in clinical priors, preventing redundant parameter expansion while maximizing knowledge reuse. Extensive evaluations across 12 sequential brain lesion MRI tasks demonstrate that CoRE achieves state-of-the-art performance and provides a high knowledge starting point for efficient future adaptation. Its superior few-shot transferability and clinical interpretability further validate its effectiveness in managing non-stationary clinical data streams. Our code will be released soon.
2604.25376.paper_summary_zh: 準確的腦部病變分割在MRI中對於有效的臨床診斷和治療計劃至關重要。由於高昂的標註成本和嚴格的數據隱私法規，通用模型需要採用持續學習（CL）來適應不斷演變的臨床任務，而不會失去先前獲得的知識。然而，現有的CL範式往往受到容量限制或冗餘參數增長的困擾，即使是先進的動態方法也主要依賴於圖像感知策略，這些策略難以處理腦部影像中固有的顯著病理和多模態異質性。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了概念推理擴展（CoRE）框架，通過將視覺特徵與結構化概念整合，建立了一個聯合決策機制。通過將圖像標記與層次概念庫對齊，CoRE 模擬臨床推理，以指導可解釋的專家路由和基於需求的模型增長。這一協作過程確保模型的演變根植於臨床先驗，防止冗餘參數擴展，同時最大化知識重用。在12個連續腦部病變MRI任務中的廣泛評估顯示，CoRE實現了最先進的性能，並為未來高效適應提供了良好的知識起點。其卓越的少樣本可轉移性和臨床可解釋性進一步驗證了其在管理非穩態臨床數據流中的有效性。我們的代碼將很快發布。
2604.25376.author: Qianqian Chen et.al.
2604.25376.authors: Qianqian Chen, Anglin Liu, Jingyang Zhang, Yudong Zhang
